description: Annual federal government budget appropriation and key figures

sources.name: Budget Office of the Federation
sources.note: Official source for Appropriation Acts. Publishes approved budgets, implementation reports, and citizen guides.

sources.name: BudgIT
sources.note: Civil society budget analysis. Publishes annual budget breakdowns, infographics, and state-level data.

sources.name: Open Treasury
sources.note: Government spending transparency portal. Shows daily treasury disbursements and MDA allocations.

data_notes.methodology: Figures are the total appropriation as signed into law (Appropriation Act). Supplementary budgets are noted separately where applicable. Figures are nominal (not inflation-adjusted).
data_notes.verification: Each figure corresponds to the signed Appropriation Act for that fiscal year. Cross-reference with Budget Office publications and BudgIT analysis.
manual_update_steps: Visit Budget Office website for latest Appropriation Act and breakdown
Download BudgIT annual budget analysis for detailed sector breakdowns
Check Open Treasury portal for execution/disbursement data
For sub-national data, check BudgIT state budget trackers

data.label: 2012 Appropriation Act
data.note: Year of partial subsidy removal and Occupy Nigeria protests

data.label: 2015 Appropriation Act
data.note: Oil price crash led to reduced budget

data.label: 2016 Appropriation Act
data.note: Budget of Change. Signed in May 2016 after prolonged legislative delays.

data.label: 2019 Appropriation Act
data.note: Budget of Continuity. Year of #EndSARS precursor protests.

data.label: 2020 Appropriation Act (revised)
data.note: Originally 10.59 TRN, revised upward due to COVID-19 supplementary budget. Year of #EndSARS protests (October 2020).

data.label: 2021 Appropriation Act
data.note: Budget of Economic Recovery. Includes supplementary.

data.label: 2022 Appropriation Act
data.note: Budget of Fiscal Sustainability. Fuel subsidy costs escalated significantly.

data.label: 2023 Appropriation Act
data.note: Transition year. Tinubu inaugurated May 29, announced subsidy removal.

data.label: 2024 Appropriation Act
data.note: Budget of Renewed Hope. Signed December 2023. First full Tinubu budget.

data.label: 2025 Appropriation Act
data.note: Budget of Restoration. Significant increase driven by naira devaluation effects and expanded capital spending plans.

data.label: 2026 Appropriation Act
data.note: Budget of Consolidation. Tinubu proposed N58.18 trillion, National Assembly approved N68.30 trillion. Includes N15.52 trillion for debt servicing and N15.25 trillion recurrent (non-debt). Exchange rate assumption: N1,400/USD.

currencyNote: All budget figures are in Nigerian Naira (NGN). Trillions = 10^12. The significant jump from 2023 onward partly reflects naira devaluation — the same USD-equivalent spending appears much larger in naira terms.
